channel.title: Open-Source on jamesm.blog
channel.description: Recent content in Open-Source on jamesm.blog

item.title: Cline: The Next Generation AI Coding Assistant
item.description: An exploration of Cline, the autonomous AI coding agent that lives in your IDE and handles complex, multi-step engineering tasks through tool-use and agency.

item.title: The Rise of Small Language Models: Why Size Isn't Everything
item.description: Why smaller language models are proving that efficiency, speed, and cost matter more than raw parameter count. A look at the shift redefining AI development.

item.title: Open WebUI: A Polished Interface for Local and Remote LLMs
item.description: Open WebUI is an open-source interface that brings ChatGPT-like convenience to local language models, giving you a sleek chat interface for Ollama, OpenAI, and compatible APIs with zero vendor lock-in.

item.title: Running AI Models Locally with Ollama: From Setup to OpenClaw
item.description: A practical guide to Ollama, the platform for running large language models locally, including how to launch OpenClaw and explore its available models.

item.title: GitHub Is Now Officially Backing OpenClaw
item.description: GitHub became an official sponsor of OpenClaw, the fastest-growing open source project in history, providing Copilot Pro+ and security funding.

item.title: Following the Money: Databricks vs Snowflake vs the Open-Source Alternative
item.description: A deep dive into the shifting economics of the data landscape in 2026. Why the choice between Snowflake and Databricks is increasingly an accounting decision, and where the open-source DIY stack actually saves you money.

item.title: List of AI GitHub Projects
item.description:
A collection of significant open-source AI projects that are shaping the ecosystem.

AI Agent Frameworks

  • AutoGen - Microsoft’s multi-agent conversation framework for building complex AI systems with role-based agents
  • CrewAI - Framework for orchestrating autonomous AI agents that work together as a crew
  • Langchain - Foundational library for building applications with LLMs, offering chains, agents, and memory abstractions
  • Open Interpreter - Let LLMs run code locally and interact with your computer

Code & Development

  • Auto-GPT - Early autonomous AI agent that can break down goals and execute them iteratively
  • Aider - AI pair programmer that can edit code in your local repository
  • Prompt Engineering Guide - Comprehensive guide with papers, techniques, and best practices

Specialized Tools

  • OpenClaw - AI agent framework for operating graphical user interfaces directly
  • Ollama - Simple way to run large language models locally
  • LiteLLM - Unified interface for calling all major LLM APIs with cost tracking

Research & Resources

  • Transformers - Hugging Face’s comprehensive library for state-of-the-art NLP models
  • Papers with Code - Curated dataset linking papers with their implementations
